ops.title: parseNamespaceOrigin truncates multi-token schemes (chrome-extension:// -> extension://)
ops.description: Pre-existing, surfaced by the codex refuter pass while fixing 3e458cf: isSchemeByte (internal/cookie/webstorage.go) walks back over ASCII letters only, so a Session Storage namespace key for a chrome-extension://<id>/ origin stops at the internal '-' and returns 'extension://<id>/' as the origin. RFC 3986 schemes allow digits/'+'/'-'/'.', but naively widening the walk set would eat the '-' guid separator and hex guid bytes, so the fix needs care (e.g. greedy backward extension across '-' while the preceding run is scheme-legal and still followed by a '-' separator; note a guid is hex-only, 'chrome' is not hex-representable... 'c' 'e' 'a' etc are, so validate against the full guid grammar rather than charset intuition). Impact today is marginal: NormalizeHost matching means a truncated extension origin only surfaces if a user requests that host shape. Regression test: namespace-<guid>-chrome-extension://abcdef/ must yield chrome-extension://abcdef/.

ops.text: parseNamespaceOrigin returns chrome-extension://<id>/ intact from a namespace key
web+foo:// style multi-token schemes covered by a table row or explicitly rejected by design
existing first-party and partitioned rows in TestParseNamespaceOriginPartitioned stay green
